# Feature #23 — TXT auto-generated TOC verification (round-4)

Closes acceptance criterion (UI render + tap-to-navigate) for feature #23.
Verifies that `TXTTocRuleEngine` extracts chapter entries from a real TXT
fixture and that the rendered TOC entries are tap-navigable in the live app.

Prior rounds were blocked by:
- Round-1 (2026-05-05): MCP harness failure
- Round-2 (2026-05-09): DebugBridge recovered but bundled stub fixture had
  no chapter content matching enabled rules
- Round-3 (implicit): feature #45 WI-2 added the XCUITest scaffold but
  initially seeded with `.books` (metadata-only stubs, no file content)

Round-4 ships a small WI-2 follow-up patch (1-line seed change in
`Feature23TXTTocVerificationTests.swift`) and runs the test against the
real `war-and-peace.txt` fixture.

## Acceptance criteria

| Criterion | Observed | Pass |
|---|---|---|
| Auto-TOC detection from chapter markers | `TXTTocRuleEngine` rule #3 (英文Chapter/Section/Part, enabled=true) matches "Chapter 1/2/3" lines in war-and-peace.txt; entries appear in panel | ✅ |
| Contents tab renders entries (not empty state) | After opening the war-and-peace book + tapping Contents tab, `tocEmptyState` is absent and at least one `tocRow-*` button exists | ✅ |
| Tap-to-navigate dismisses panel + keeps reader loaded | Tapping the first TOC row dismisses the `annotationsPanelSheet` and the reader's back button remains visible | ✅ |

## Commands run

```bash
DEVELOPER_DIR=/Applications/Xcode.app/Contents/Developer xcodebuild test \
  -project vreader.xcodeproj -scheme vreader \
  -destination 'platform=iOS Simulator,name=iPhone 17 Pro' \
  -only-testing:vreaderUITests/Feature23TXTTocVerificationTests/verify_feature_23_txt_toc_populated_for_chapters \
  -only-testing:vreaderUITests/Feature23TXTTocVerificationTests/verify_feature_23_txt_toc_navigation_jumps_to_chapter
```

Result: `** TEST SUCCEEDED **` — 2 tests, 0 failures, 25.97 s wall-clock
on iPhone 17 Pro Simulator (iOS 26.5, v3.21.4 build 281).

## Observations

- **Round-3 → Round-4 diagnostic chain**: round-3 (this morning's verify
  cron) ran the just-merged WI-2 test against `.books` seed, surfaced the
  XCTAssertTrue failure at line 95, root-caused to seed mismatch, posted
  the finding to GH #576. This iteration ships the corrective 1-line
  patch (`.books` → `.warAndPeace`) and re-runs the test to convert the
  Round-3 finding into a Round-4 pass.
- **Why the original WI-2 test seed was wrong**: `.books` seeds
  `BookRecord` stubs via `TestSeeder.makeRecord(...)` — these have
  metadata only (no backing file in `ImportedBooks/`), so the TXT reader
  cannot load real content when tapped. `tocEmptyState` doesn't render
  (because TOC generation never runs), and no `tocRow-*` either.
  `.warAndPeace` seeds a single real TXT book with the bundled fixture's
  file content, allowing the TOC pipeline to execute.
- **Rule #3 efficacy**: the English regex correctly matches lines like
  "Chapter 1" even without a trailing title (the `.{0,30}$` tail allows
  empty match).
- **No regression in WI-1/WI-2 deliverables**: this is an additive
  seed change, no production code touched. The 3 other WI-2 tests
  (#21/#27/#28) continue to use `.books` since they exercise UI surfaces
  that don't require book content.

- xcresult bundle:
  `~/Library/Developer/Xcode/DerivedData/vreader-*/Logs/Test/Test-vreader-2026.05.13_06-35-29-+0800.xcresult`
  (system-local; not committed)
- No screenshot artifacts captured this round — the test ran headlessly
  via xcodebuild test with no XCTAttachment screenshot calls. The
  passing test outcome is the authoritative evidence for the row flip.
